title: Add two-phase app-interface submit/ACK contract for compatibility transports
description: Code review during project-LICHEN-9c4y.22 found that MeshCore and existing channel text paths submit to lichen_app_interface before the compatibility OK response is actually enqueued. preflight_tx_slots catches ordinary backpressure but cannot roll back a submit if enqueue_tx fails because of a BLE session/queue race after submit. Design and implement a reserve/commit/abort or otherwise rollback-capable app-interface/message-contract path so compatibility transports can guarantee either (a) no app-interface message is committed unless the client receives OK, or (b) a precise documented at-least-once/error semantic with tests. Cover MeshCore direct text, MeshCore channel text, Meshtastic text ingress if affected, and gateway message_contract tests.
notes:
Implementation slice before EC2 validation:
- Documented message_contract submit as an at-least-once app ingress boundary rather than a BLE ACK boundary. If a MeshCore/Meshtastic session drops after app-interface submit but before OK/status enqueue, the message remains committed and the dispatcher returns the transport enqueue error.
- Added MeshCore gateway tests for channel text and direct peer text using fake_ble_meshcore_disconnect_on_next_enqueue(); both assert -ENOTCONN, zero TX frames, and retained message_contract payload.
- Added Meshtastic gateway tests for direct peer text and primary-channel/broadcast text using fake_ble_meshtastic_disconnect_on_next_enqueue(); both assert -ENOTCONN, zero FromRadio frames, and retained message_contract payload.

Review evidence:
- Codereview agent 019f2430-4d54-7511-9e7b-d7ee5d392fa5: no P0/P1/P2 findings; verified documented semantic matches current submit-before-ACK flow and new MeshCore/Meshtastic coverage.
- Codereview agent 019f2430-5e94-7293-9dad-7492e607d195: no P0/P1/P2 findings; verified fake BLE hooks reproduce post-submit enqueue failure and assertions match gateway APIs.

Local checks:
- git diff --check: pass.
- rg confirmed added disconnect-after-submit tests and at-least-once contract text.

- Note: launched with minimal user-data because aws-builder-user-data.sh is on the unattached EBS; after attach/mount, ran /mnt/lichen-zephyr/scripts/bootstrap-host.sh and sourced /mnt/lichen-zephyr/env.sh.
- Corrected validation clone to fetch GitHub main directly because cached local origin was stale; validated commit b611ccd.
- Command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshcore_gateway_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-9c4y221 --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen
- Result: 3 scenarios/instances selected; 2 executed configurations passed, 1 skipped by static filter, 0 failed, 0 errored, 0 warnings, 72 test cases executed, 50 skipped, elapsed 50.77s.

close_reason: Completed no-hardware compatibility submit/ACK contract work by documenting the at-least-once app-ingress/error semantic, adding MeshCore and Meshtastic gateway disconnect-race tests, completing two independent code reviews with no P0/P1/P2 findings, and validating affected native_sim tests on EC2 Twister.
